data.title: Exponent Maintained at Buy as Strong Q1 Beat and Durable Growth Drivers Support Unchanged $66.89 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Andrew Nicholas of William Blair reiterated a Buy rating on Exponent, retaining the price target of $66.89.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Nicholas’s rating is based on Exponent’s strong first-quarter outperformance and the durability of its growth drivers. The company exceeded his expectations on net revenue, profitability, and earnings per share, while management maintained full-year guidance, reinforcing confidence in the outlook and justifying his unchanged $66.89 price target.

Andrew Nicholas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of robust demand across both reactive and proactive services and clear benefits from AI-related work. He highlights especially strong momentum in consumer electronics, diversified across health-related devices, AI-enabled products, and hardware, which, alongside secular tailwinds and a reduced revenue concentration, should help smooth out cyclical swings and support continued double-digit top-line growth.

data.title: Five9 Buy Rating Reaffirmed as Fundamentals Improve and AI Growth Accelerates; $40 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Analyst Scott Berg of Needham maintained a Buy rating on Five9, retaining the price target of $40.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Scott Berg has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that indicate Five9’s fundamentals are improving and growth is reaccelerating. The company delivered strong first-quarter results, with AI-related revenue surging year over year and key demand metrics, such as long-term dollar-based retention, inflecting positively after a prolonged decline.

He also highlights that subscription revenue growth has stabilized, management under new leadership is providing greater operational transparency, and a larger share repurchase program underscores confidence in near-term performance and free-cash-flow per share. Additionally, guidance calling for faster revenue expansion in FY26, alongside robust expected AI growth, supports the view that demand trends remain healthy and justify maintaining a $40 price target.

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on MGM Amid Structural Headwinds and Muted Growth Prospects Despite Solid Execution
data.description:
MGM Resorts, the Consumer Cyclical sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst David Katz from Jefferies downgraded the rating on the stock to a Hold and gave it a $44.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Katz has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors that temper the stock’s upside potential despite solid operational execution. He views MGM’s complex operating and property-ownership structure, combined with fully leased U.S. assets, as a drag on long‑term earnings durability and a key reason the shares warrant a valuation discount relative to faster-growing peers.

Katz also highlights muted growth drivers, citing choppy Las Vegas leisure trends, tougher year‑over‑year comparisons in Macau, and the long lead time before Japan can contribute meaningfully. While buybacks, digital initiatives such as Brazil, and disciplined capital deployment could eventually support a re‑rating, he sees more immediate and compelling opportunities in other land‑based gaming names, justifying a neutral stance for now.

In another report released yesterday, Barclays also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $39.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Atlassian Earns Buy Rating on Accelerating Cloud Growth, AI Momentum, and $1B+ Services ARR Strengthening Long-Term Outlook
data.description:
William Blair analyst Arjun Bhatia has maintained their bullish stance on TEAM stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Arjun Bhatia has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Atlassian’s accelerating cloud performance and durable demand drivers. Cloud revenue growth has re-accelerated well above market expectations, supported by robust paid seat additions, strong enterprise adoption, and healthy migration from Data Center to cloud, giving management confidence to raise forward guidance.

Bhatia also points to the rapid momentum of Rovo and Atlassian’s broader AI initiatives, which are spurring higher ARR growth, rising AI credit usage, and upgrades to higher-value bundles. In addition, the Service Collection has surpassed $1 billion in ARR while sustaining strong growth, including notable competitive wins and especially rapid expansion in enterprise service, reinforcing the long-term growth and competitive positioning that underpin his Buy stance.

In another report released today, KeyBanc also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $130.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on DUG, Maintains $3.20 Price Target on Strong Growth, Sector Tailwinds and Middle East Seismic Opportunity
data.description:
PAC Partners analyst Caleb Weng has maintained their bullish stance on DUG stock, giving a Buy rating on April 14.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Caleb Weng has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including DUG’s solid recent performance and favourable industry dynamics. He notes that the company has delivered strong revenue and EBITDA growth, with robust margins, while its orderbook, though flat, is sufficient to support earnings at the upper end of FY26 market expectations.

He also highlights a strengthening demand backdrop, as major oil companies are increasing exploration budgets and sector peers signal rising imaging revenues into 2026. In addition, a sizeable upcoming seismic data project in the Middle East points to a substantial medium-term opportunity for processing and imaging work, and when combined with DUG’s reasonable FY27 valuation multiple, this underpins his conviction in maintaining a Buy rating and a $3.20 price target.

In another report released on April 14, Ord Minnett also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a A$2.93 price target.
